246	// WakeProject signals every running execute-loop worker bound to projectRoot
247	// to skip its current idle-poll sleep and re-scan the ready queue. Used by
248	// the operator-prompt approve / auto-approve mutations (Story 15) so a
249	// freshly-approved bead is claimed immediately rather than after a full
250	// PollInterval. The send is non-blocking and the per-worker wake channel is
251	// buffered (cap 1), so multiple wakes within one tick coalesce. Returns the
252	// number of workers signalled (0 when no execute-loop is running for the
253	// project — submit/approve still succeeds, the next tick on a future worker
254	// will pick the bead up).
255	func (m *WorkerManager) WakeProject(projectRoot string) int {
256		m.mu.Lock()
257		defer m.mu.Unlock()
258		signalled := 0
259		for _, h := range m.workers {
260			if h == nil || h.wakeCh == nil {
261				continue
262			}
263			if h.record.ProjectRoot != projectRoot {
264				continue
265			}
266			select {
267			case h.wakeCh <- struct{}{}:
268				signalled++
269			default:
270				// Wake already pending — coalesce.
271				signalled++
272			}
273		}
274		return signalled
275	}

306	// applyServerWatchdogConfig reads .ddx/config.yaml at projectRoot and applies
307	// any server.watchdog_deadline / server.stall_deadline overrides. Invalid or
308	// missing values are silently ignored — defaults are filled in by the
309	// watchdog loop at runtime.
310	func (m *WorkerManager) applyServerWatchdogConfig(projectRoot string) {
311		cfg, err := config.LoadWithWorkingDir(projectRoot)
312		if err != nil || cfg == nil || cfg.Server == nil {
313			return
314		}
315		if d, err := time.ParseDuration(cfg.Server.WatchdogDeadline); err == nil && d > 0 {
316			m.WatchdogDeadline = d
317		}
318		if d, err := time.ParseDuration(cfg.Server.StallDeadline); err == nil && d > 0 {
319			m.StallDeadline = d
320		}
321	}

345	func (m *WorkerManager) StartExecuteLoop(spec ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec) (WorkerRecord, error) {
346		// Resolve the effective project root: spec override takes priority over the
347		// manager's default so callers can target any registered project.
348		effectiveRoot := spec.ProjectRoot
349		if effectiveRoot == "" {
350			effectiveRoot = m.projectRoot
351		}
352	
353		// Pre-flight: validate harness availability and model compatibility
354		// before creating the worker record or claiming any beads.
355		// Skipped when OpaquePassthrough=true (ddx work path): routing belongs to
356		// the agent service; DDx must not pre-resolve or validate the route.
357		if !spec.OpaquePassthrough {
358			if err := agent.ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService(context.Background(), effectiveRoot, spec.Harness, spec.Model, spec.Provider, spec.ModelRef); err != nil {
359				return WorkerRecord{}, fmt.Errorf("execute-loop: %w", err)
360			}
361		}
362	
363		if err := os.MkdirAll(m.rootDir, 0o755); err != nil {
364			return WorkerRecord{}, err
365		}
366	
367		id := "worker-" + time.Now().UTC().Format("20060102T150405") + "-" + randomSuffix(4)
368		dir := filepath.Join(m.rootDir, id)
369		if err := os.MkdirAll(dir, 0o755); err != nil {
370			return WorkerRecord{}, err
371		}
372	
373		// Write spec
374		specData, _ := json.MarshalIndent(spec, "", "  ")
375		_ = os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(dir, "spec.json"), append(specData, '\n'), 0o644)
376	
377		// Open log file
378		logPath := filepath.Join(dir, "worker.log")
379		logFile, err := os.OpenFile(logPath, os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY|os.O_TRUNC, 0o644)
380		if err != nil {
381			return WorkerRecord{}, err
382		}
383	
384		// Open structured event sink — loop milestones (bead.claimed, bead.result,
385		// loop.start/end) land here as JSONL so log aggregators and future server
386		// endpoints can parse them independently of the human-readable worker.log.
387		eventsPath := filepath.Join(dir, "worker-events.jsonl")
388		eventsFile, eventsErr := os.OpenFile(eventsPath, os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY|os.O_TRUNC, 0o644)
389		if eventsErr != nil {
390			eventsFile = nil // non-fatal; structured events silently disabled
391		}
392	
393		record := WorkerRecord{
394			ID:           id,
395			Kind:         "execute-loop",
396			State:        "running",
397			Status:       "running",
398			ProjectRoot:  effectiveRoot,
399			Harness:      spec.Harness,
400			Model:        spec.Model,
401			Profile:      agent.NormalizeRoutingProfile(spec.Profile),
402			Effort:       spec.Effort,
403			Once:         spec.Once,
404			PollInterval: spec.PollInterval.String(),
405			StdoutPath:   relToProject(m.projectRoot, logPath),
406			SpecPath:     relToProject(m.projectRoot, filepath.Join(dir, "spec.json")),
407			StartedAt:    time.Now().UTC(),
408		}
409		record.Lifecycle = append(record.Lifecycle, WorkerLifecycleEvent{
410			Action:    "start",
411			Actor:     "local-operator",
412			Timestamp: record.StartedAt,
413			Detail:    lifecycleStartDetail(spec),
414		})
415		_ = m.writeRecord(dir, record)
416	
417		ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
418		logBuf := &bytes.Buffer{}
419		multiLog := io.MultiWriter(logBuf, logFile)
420	
421		progressCh := make(chan agent.ProgressEvent, 64)
422		wakeCh := make(chan struct{}, 1)
423		handle := &workerHandle{
424			record:       record,
425			cancel:       cancel,
426			logBuf:       logBuf,
427			logFile:      logFile,
428			progressCh:   progressCh,
429			progressDone: make(chan struct{}),
430			lastPhaseTS:  time.Now().UTC(),
431			wakeCh:       wakeCh,
432		}
433	
434		m.mu.Lock()
435		m.workers[id] = handle
436		m.mu.Unlock()
437	
438		m.ensureWatchdog()
439	
440		go m.drainProgress(id, handle, progressCh)
441		go m.runWorker(ctx, id, dir, spec, effectiveRoot, handle, multiLog, eventsFile, progressCh)
442	
443		return record, nil
444	}

11	// newWorkCommand creates the top-level "ddx work" command: the FEAT-010 layer-3
12	// queue drain. Unlike "ddx agent execute-loop", ddx work treats --harness,
13	// --provider, and --model as opaque passthrough constraints that are forwarded
14	// to the agent unchanged. DDx does not validate or branch on their values.
15	// Retry-power policy between attempts is owned here, not in the agent service.
16	func (f *CommandFactory) newWorkCommand() *cobra.Command {
17		cmd := &cobra.Command{
18			Use:   "work",
19			Short: "Drain the bead execution queue",
20			Long: `work drains the execution-ready bead queue. It is the FEAT-010 layer-3
21	queue drain: it iterates ddx try (layer 2) across ready beads until a stop
22	condition is met and owns retry-power policy between attempts.
23	
24	Unlike "ddx agent execute-loop", ddx work treats --harness, --provider, and
25	--model as opaque passthrough constraints forwarded to the agent unchanged.
26	DDx does not validate these values or branch on them; the agent owns routing
27	within the requested power bounds.
28	
29	Stop conditions (evaluated between attempts):
30	  drained     — no ready beads remain
31	  blocked     — every remaining bead has produced a terminal non-success outcome
32	  deferred    — configured budget exhausted
33	  no_progress — N consecutive attempts produced no commit (default N=3)
34	  signal      — SIGINT/SIGTERM received between attempts
35	
36	work runs inline in the current process; per ADR-022 there is no separate
37	"submit to server" mode. The legacy --local flag is accepted but ignored
38	(deprecation warning printed) and will be removed in a future release.
39	`,
40			Example: `  # Drain the current execution-ready queue once and exit
41	  ddx work
42	
43	  # Pick one ready bead, execute it, and stop
44	  ddx work --once
45	
46	  # Run continuously as a bounded queue worker
47	  ddx work --poll-interval 30s
48	
49	  # Forward harness/model as passthrough constraints (ddx does not validate these)
50	  ddx work --once --harness agent --model minimax/minimax-m2.7
51	
52	  # Constrain power tier (retry-power policy is owned by ddx work)
53	  ddx work --once --min-power 40 --max-power 90`,
54			Args: cobra.NoArgs,
55			RunE: f.runWork,
56		}
57	
58		// Routing knobs — passthrough only. ddx work forwards these to the agent
59		// unchanged and does not validate or branch on their string values.
60		cmd.Flags().String("project", "", "Target project root path or name (default: CWD git root). Env: DDX_PROJECT_ROOT")
61		cmd.Flags().String("from", "", "Base git revision to start from (default: HEAD)")
62		cmd.Flags().String("harness", "", "Agent harness constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
63		cmd.Flags().String("model", "", "Model constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
64		cmd.Flags().String("profile", agent.DefaultRoutingProfile, "Routing profile: default, cheap, fast, or smart")
65		cmd.Flags().String("provider", "", "Provider constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
66		cmd.Flags().String("model-ref", "", "Model catalog reference (e.g. code-medium); resolved via the model catalog")
67		cmd.Flags().String("effort", "", "Effort level")
68		cmd.Flags().Bool("once", false, "Process at most one ready bead")
69		cmd.Flags().Duration("poll-interval", 30*time.Second, "Poll interval for continuous scanning; zero drains current ready work and exits (legacy opt-out). Default 30s keeps the worker alive across empty polls.")
70		cmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "Output loop result as JSON")
71		cmd.Flags().Bool("local", false, "Deprecated: no-op; ddx work always runs inline (ADR-022)")
72		_ = cmd.Flags().MarkDeprecated("local", "ddx work always runs inline; the flag is a no-op (ADR-022)")
73		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-review", false, "Skip post-merge review")
74		cmd.Flags().String("review-harness", "", "Harness for the post-merge reviewer (default: same as implementation harness)")
75		cmd.Flags().String("review-model", "", "Model override for the post-merge reviewer (default: smart tier)")
76		cmd.Flags().Float64("max-cost", escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD, "Stop when accumulated billed cost exceeds USD; 0 = unlimited")
77		cmd.Flags().Duration("request-timeout", 0, "Per-request provider wall-clock timeout; overrides project config and model-class defaults")
78		// Power bounds: ddx work owns retry-power policy — these set the envelope
79		// for operator-constrained power within which the agent selects the route.
80		cmd.Flags().Int("min-power", 0, "Minimum model power required (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged")
81		cmd.Flags().Int("max-power", 0, "Maximum model power allowed (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged")
82	
83		return cmd
84	}

86	// runWork is the RunE for "ddx work". It drives the FEAT-010 layer-3 queue
87	// drain with harness/provider/model treated as opaque passthrough constraints —
88	// ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService is not called; DDx does not inspect or
89	// branch on those values. Retry-power policy (min-power / max-power) is owned
90	// by this layer.
91	func (f *CommandFactory) runWork(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
92		return f.runAgentExecuteLoopImpl(cmd, true)
93	}

status: needs_investigation
reason: Bead scope significantly exceeds the stated "~200 LOC + test updates" estimate; safe execution requires decomposition into 3 child beads, but child-bead creation must originate from the main repo (not this execution worktree) to get clean ddx-prefixed IDs.

## Why this attempt declined to commit

The bead asks for an end-to-end migration of the server-spawn path from in-process execution to subprocess `exec ddx work`. Six tightly-coupled ACs touch the most intricate file in the server package.

### Actual surface (vs stated estimate)

cli/internal/server/workers.go is 1,959 lines. The current `runWorker` (lines 612–926, ~315 lines) is a dense in-process orchestrator that knits together:

- in-process `agent.ExecuteBeadWorker` construction
- per-project `LandCoordinator` single-flight serialization
- in-process post-merge `DefaultBeadReviewer`
- in-process `escalation.CostCapTracker` (per-worker billed cost accumulation)
- in-process `RoutePreflight` (typed-incompatibility gate via FizeauService)
- per-attempt `singleTierAttempt` closure with config resolution + gate evaluation + leftover-evidence safety net
- progress channel piped to SSE subscribers via `drainProgress`
- watchdog phase-stall tracking via shared workerHandle.lastPhaseTS
- terminal-state preservation (stopped/reaped) merged with loop result
- BeadWorkerFactory injection point for test fakes

Replacing the in-process call with `exec ddx work` collapses all of those in-process couplings — they now run inside the child process — but the parent server still needs equivalent observability/control via:

- /api/workers/{id}/event ingestion (already exists from step 1) for progress
- PID + signal escalation for watchdog/Stop (PID field exists; signal plumbing is partial)
- worker-events.jsonl tail or HTTP-mirrored events for SSE bridging
- Cross-process Stop / wakeCh equivalent

### Test surface

cli/internal/server/workers_test.go alone is 1,107 lines, plus 9 sibling workers_*_test.go files and graphql_subscription_worker_test.go that all assert in-process semantics. Many tests inject `BeadWorkerFactory` for control over the in-process worker; with subprocess execution, those tests need to be replaced by either:

- a built-in subprocess-stub binary (e.g. `go test -test.run … -ddx-fake-work-stub` reentry pattern) the parent execs in tests, or
- deletion as no-longer-applicable (semantics now belong to ddx work's own test surface).

This is the bulk of the work, not "test updates."

### AC #5 (real-harness end-to-end)

"server-spawned worker (via the new exec path) registers via the new endpoints AND successfully invokes the claude harness (real claude binary, not a stub) on a fixture bead AND completes the attempt with a commit"

Even with the fall-back to a scripted-claude-stub, this requires: a clean fixture repo with a real ready bead, a stub or real claude binary on $PATH inside the test harness, a server bound to a free port, the new exec path wired in, the worker registering against the running server's /api/workers/register, and an end-to-end commit landing through the LandCoordinator (which has moved out of the parent process). That is a substantial new integration test — comparable to the workers_e2e_test.go suite — distinct from the refactor proper.

### Size-check verdict

- More than ~6 ACs across unrelated subsystems? 6 ACs, but several (in-process-replacement refactor, struct deletion, full test sweep, real-harness integration) are independently sized engineering deliverables.
- AC mixes implementation, integration tests, and end-to-end scenarios? Yes (AC #1–4 = refactor, AC #5 = new integration test, AC #6 = full lefthook gate).
- More than ~500 LOC across more than ~5 files? Yes — workers.go rewrite (~400), server.go handler (~70), workers_test.go sweep (~600+), sibling test sweep (~500+), new exec/stub plumbing (~200), new e2e integration test (~300). Estimated 1,500–2,500 LOC of changes.

The bead's "~200 LOC + test updates" estimate (echoing the ADR-022 step 7 roadmap line) materially understates the work.

## Proposed decomposition (orchestrator should create from main repo)

Create the following children of ddx-eccc6efb from the **main repo** (not from an execution worktree — IDs created from a worktree get the worktree dirname as the project prefix and become unusable):

### 7a — runWorker exec rewrite + parent-side subprocess plumbing
Title: ADR-022 step 7a: workers.go runWorker → exec ddx work subprocess (progress via /api/workers/{id}/event, watchdog via PID)
Scope:
- Replace the in-process `worker.Run(...)` body in cli/internal/server/workers.go with `exec.CommandContext` of `ddx work` (resolved via os.Executable() + arg-vec flags + DDX_PROJECT_ROOT env).
- Record child PID on WorkerRecord; ensure watchdog SIGTERM/SIGKILL via PID still works.
- Bridge child-emitted progress events into the existing parent SSE subscriber path — either by POST to /api/workers/{id}/event being multiplexed back to the in-memory progressCh, or by tailing the child's worker-events.jsonl.
- Stop()/cancel/wake plumbing: Stop signals the PID; wakeCh is replaced by sending a SIGUSR1 (or equivalent) handled by ddx work, OR documented as no-op when subprocess.
- Drop in-process LandCoordinator coupling, in-process DefaultBeadReviewer, in-process CostCapTracker, in-process RoutePreflight from this code path.
- Update workers_test.go, workers_costcap_test.go, workers_gates_test.go, workers_watchdog_test.go, workers_wake_test.go, workers_reap_unix.go, workers_stop_test.go, workers_stop_propagation_test.go, workers_e2e_test.go, graphql_subscription_worker_test.go to use a subprocess-stub binary (or test-binary reentry) instead of BeadWorkerFactory injection.
- handleStartExecuteLoopWorker untouched at this point — still uses ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec.
Acceptance: subprocess exec path works; existing test scenarios pass against subprocess-stub; lefthook pre-commit green.
Estimated: ~800–1,000 LOC.

### 7b — handler migration + ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec deletion
Title: ADR-022 step 7b: handleStartExecuteLoopWorker → exec ddx work; delete ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec
Scope:
- handleStartExecuteLoopWorker decodes HTTP request → builds argv/env → calls a thinner StartExecuteLoop(opts) that no longer takes the legacy ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec struct.
- Delete ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec struct and its JSON marshalling.
- Update server_test.go, bounded_egress_test.go, federation_*_test.go, and any other call sites that constructed an ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec literal.
Acceptance: spec struct gone; handler tests assert exec form; full ./internal/server/... + ./cmd/... green.
Estimated: ~300–500 LOC.

### 7c — End-to-end integration test (scripted-claude-stub or opt-in real claude)
Title: ADR-022 step 7c: end-to-end test — server-spawned worker via exec path registers, invokes harness on fixture bead, lands commit
Scope: Build the AC #5 evidence the parent bead requires:
- Spin up a server.Server in-test bound to an ephemeral port.
- Use scripts/build-fixture-repo.sh (federated profile) for a clean ddx-initialized git repo with one ready bead.
- POST /api/agent/workers/execute-loop with --once.
- Assert the child ddx work process registers via POST /api/workers/register, claims the bead, the scripted-claude-stub (matching the same exec args + env-var path real claude uses) emits an evidence file, the attempt commits, and the bead closes (or preserves with a known-good status).
- Optional: gate a separate test on `DDX_E2E_REAL_CLAUDE=1` that runs the same scenario against a real `claude` binary on $PATH.
Acceptance: green CI run with scripted-claude-stub end-to-end; opt-in real-claude variant documented.
Estimated: ~400–600 LOC + fixture infra.

### Dependencies
- 7b dep 7a (handler migration assumes runWorker already speaks exec)
- 7c dep 7b (end-to-end via the new exec path requires the new handler shape)

## What blocked this attempt from a clean partial commit

The 6 ACs are tightly interlocking — partial implementation (e.g. just runWorker rewrite without handler migration, or rewrite without test sweep) leaves the package in a non-compiling or red-test state (BeadWorkerFactory injection points either still exist and are dead, or are removed and break tests). A clean intermediate slice requires the explicit decomposition above so each child commits a green tree.

## Bookkeeping

A child bead was inadvertently created from this worktree before the orchestrator-must-be-main-repo issue was identified; its ID `.execute-bead-wt-ddx-eccc6efb-20260503T065047-61e4ed20-c4de2d6e` is malformed and the bead has been closed. The orchestrator should disregard it and create the three children above from the main repo where the project prefix resolves to `ddx-`.

data.text:
I declined to attempt this bead and wrote `.ddx/executions/20260503T065047-61e4ed20/no_changes_rationale.txt` with `status: needs_investigation` plus a proposed 3-child decomposition (7a runWorker exec rewrite + parent-side plumbing + test sweep, 7b handler migration + spec deletion, 7c end-to-end with scripted-claude-stub or opt-in real claude).

Reasons in brief:
- workers.go is 1,959 lines; `runWorker` (~315 lines) intricately knits in-process LandCoordinator, post-merge reviewer, CostCapTracker, RoutePreflight, progress→SSE, watchdog phase tracking, and BeadWorkerFactory injection — replacing it with `exec ddx work` requires equivalent cross-process plumbing, not a drop-in swap.
- Test surface alone (workers_test.go = 1,107 lines + 9 sibling workers_*_test.go files + graphql_subscription_worker_test.go) is mostly built around in-process `BeadWorkerFactory` injection — needs a subprocess-stub re-architecture.
- AC #5 (real-harness or scripted-claude-stub end-to-end through the new exec path) is itself a substantial new integration suite.
- Estimated 1,500–2,500 LOC across refactor + test sweep + new e2e — vs the bead's "~200 LOC + test updates" estimate carried over from the ADR-022 roadmap line.

Bookkeeping note: a child bead was created from this worktree before I realized worktree-originated `ddx bead create` produces a mangled project prefix (`.execute-bead-wt-…-c4de2d6e`); I closed it. The orchestrator should create the three proposed children from the main repo so they get clean `ddx-` IDs.
